eligibilityStates.meaning: visible-use output exists, but final human artifact and downstream workflow settlement are not both authoritative
the case cannot become eligible until adjudication resolves risk, conflict, policy, or counterfactual uncertainty
settled, quality-accepted, non-excluded, non-revoked feedback may be used by governed learning or evaluation flows
the visible-use artifact is not trainable because a deterministic exclusion reason applies before eligibility
a previously eligible flag is superseded by a later incident, supersession, policy, integrity, or consent event

objectContracts.objectRole: settlement-backed trainability gate for visible-use assistive artifacts
dependencyRules: eligible requires a settled finalHumanArtifactRef and authoritativeWorkflowSettlementRef
eligible requires labelQualityState of routine_clean or adjudicated_clean
eligible requires counterfactualCompletenessState of complete or not_applicable
eligible is forbidden when any exclusionReasonRefs or unresolved incidentRefs exist
revoked creates a new flag that supersedes the old eligible flag instead of mutating it in place

objectContracts.objectRole: repeatable derivation record for a FeedbackEligibilityFlag decision
dependencyRules: decisionState passed may emit eligible only through FeedbackEligibilityFlag
all check refs must be frozen refs, not current mutable task fields

objectContracts.objectRole: deterministic routing object for feedback cases that need separate human judgement
dependencyRules: dual-review, high-risk, policy-exception, and incident-linked cases require minimumReviewerCount of at least two
clinicalSafetyLeadRequired is true for high-risk, incident-linked, and safety-critical taxonomy records
settled_clean may unlock eligibility only after settlement and exclusion checks pass

objectContracts.objectRole: append-only record explaining why a previously eligible flag stopped being trainable
dependencyRules: published revocation records must point to a replacement FeedbackEligibilityFlag in revoked state
revocation supersedes prior eligibility and preserves the old flag address
training exports must consume the replacement flag state

objectContracts.objectRole: versioned policy table for deterministic feedback exclusion and revocation routing
dependencyRules: FeedbackEligibilityEvaluation must bind one published TrainabilityExclusionPolicy
policy changes never rewrite historical eligibility flags
policy_exception_override must route to adjudication or exclusion before eligibility

eligibilityRequirements: finalHumanArtifactRef is settled and points to the human-owned final artifact
authoritativeWorkflowSettlementRef confirms downstream workflow outcome is settled
incidentSearchRef has no open or later-linked incident
supersessionSearchRef has no later final-human-artifact supersession
policyExclusionCheckRef has no active exclusion reason

exclusionReasons.meaning: a policy exception was used and must not silently become training data
local law requires more than one reviewer before feedback can be eligible
clinical or safety risk class requires senior review before eligibility
the case is linked to an incident or safety investigation
the visible interaction produced regenerated draft material without stable final acceptance lineage
the user action occurred under recovery posture or stale publication/runtime truth
required rejected, changed, or alternative outcome evidence is incomplete
a later human artifact superseded the artifact that would have made the feedback trainable
the assistive suggestion was dismissed and has no accepted final artifact lineage
final artifact or downstream workflow settlement is absent
information-governance, consent, or objection rule bars trainability
label quality has not reached routine_clean or adjudicated_clean
the case combines acceptance with low confidence or safety-relevant uncertainty
ground-truth labels conflict and need separate adjudication truth

revocationReasons.meaning: a safety, quality, or information-governance incident was linked after eligibility
a later final human artifact superseded the accepted artifact
adjudication reversed a previously clean label or feedback decision
a later policy version or exception review bars trainability
counterfactual or rejection evidence was found incomplete or corrupt
information-governance or patient objection rules revoke training use
a recalled prompt, model, or dataset cut invalidates the prior eligibility

trainingExportRules: only FeedbackEligibilityFlag.state eligible may feed governed training export
requires_adjudication, pending_settlement, excluded, and revoked are non-trainable
revoked always wins over a historical eligible flag
training export must carry the flag id, policy version, and replay bundle hash

invariants.statement: FeedbackEligibilityFlag cannot be eligible without settled final human artifact and authoritative workflow settlement.
Adjudication routing is deterministic from reason codes and policy version.
Exclusion before eligibility and revocation after eligibility are separate histories.
A revoked flag may not be exported as trainable even if an older flag was eligible.
